title: Forest sub-canopy waypoint phase
summary: The forest vertical family now has enough route shape; the next gain should turn two existing cue anchors into quiet places. This wave adds one cave-mouth observation point around `stone-basin-return-light` and one canopy rest nook around `old-growth-inner-rest-light`, using the live forest roster and current recovery seams instead of more height or depth.

execution_order.why_now: Forest vertical spaces feel like corridors rather than habitats.
Named rest points turn traversal into exploration with ecological teaching.
Waypoint placement needs spatial and science review.

guardrails: Waypoints should be rest-and-observe spots, not progression gates.
Canopy nook should host epiphyte or canopy-specialist authored entities.
Cave-mouth point should teach transition between light and dark zones.
Must not alter existing traversal routes or zone boundaries.
Stay within the existing giant-tree and cave footprint.
Do not add new climbables or raise the route above the current crown-rest ceiling.
